title: Review: ‘Supergirl’ is a hero in search of a better movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Milly Alcock stars as Supergirl in a film criticized for being bland and derivative.
Supergirl's story involves a mission of vengeance that riffs on 'True Grit' but lacks originality.
Flashbacks enrich Supergirl's character, but poor story structure limits emotional impact.

title: One of Cambridge’s most beloved Japanese restaurants is closing after 34 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Tampopo, a beloved Japanese restaurant in Cambridge, will close after 34 years as owner Yasumasa Ito retires.
Located at 1815 Massachusetts Ave., Tampopo will serve its last meal on June 30.
Former employee Kotaro Morita returned from Japan to assist during the restaurant's final weeks.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Wide receivers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown joins the Patriots, replacing Stefon Diggs as their top wide receiver, aiming to enhance their passing attack.
The Patriots' wide receiver corps includes A.J. Brown, Romeo Doubs, Mack Hollins, Kayshon Boutte, and others heading into training camp.
Despite concerns over Brown's knee, he showed promising performance in minicamp, with Boutte facing potential trade speculation.

title: Here’s the Celtics’ reported asking price for Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics reportedly demand four first-round picks for Jaylen Brown in trade discussions.
Boston failed to trade Brown in a package for Giannis Antetokounmpo with the Bucks.
Brown, 29, still has three years on his Celtics contract after a strong season.

title: Norway vs. France live updates: French still leading 3-1 in back-and-forth battle at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France leads Norway 3-1 in World Cup Group I match at Gillette Stadium, Foxborough.
Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick for France, including goals in the 7th and 21st minutes.
Norwegian striker Erling Haaland is benched as team rests several starters for this match.

title: When Pickles the dog saved the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: In 1966, a dog named Pickles found the stolen Jules Rimet Trophy in London, saving the World Cup.
Pickles became a national hero in England, receiving a reward and attending celebratory events as a special guest.
The Jules Rimet Trophy was ultimately stolen again in 1983 in Brazil and has not been recovered since.

title: More than 4,000 Mass General Brigham nurses to strike on July 8
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Approximately 4,500 nurses and clinicians at Mass General Brigham plan to strike on July 8 seeking better labor agreements.
The strike includes over 4,000 nurses at Brigham and Women's Hospital and 450 clinicians from Mass General Brigham Home Care.
Mass General Brigham states nurses are highly compensated, offering a 5% wage increase and competitive benefits amidst strike preparations.

title: Wellesley man pleads not guilty to stabbing his father to death at playground
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: James Floyd Bennett III pleaded not guilty to first-degree murder in his father's stabbing at a Wellesley playground.
Prosecutors say James Bennett Jr. died from 20 stab wounds after playing soccer with his son, who later turned himself in.
James Bennett III's attorney cited his client's history of mental health issues; a probable cause hearing is set for July 10.

title: Young boy hospitalized after falling from second-floor window in Worcester
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 4-year-old boy fell from a second-story window on Southold Road, Worcester, Thursday evening, according to police.
The boy sustained bumps and bruises and is in stable condition at a local hospital, officials confirmed.
Worcester Police Department is investigating the circumstances of the fall, with no further details released yet.

title: 12-year-old dead, another child injured after bus collides with motorized scooter in Haverhill
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 12-year-old girl died and another child was injured in a bus collision with a motorized scooter in Haverhill.
The crash occurred at Portland Street and 5th Avenue, with police responding before 4 p.m. on Thursday.
Haverhill schools' Superintendent Marotta urged community support and highlighted risks of unregulated micromobility devices.
